{{Infobox location
| name = Naraka
| introduced = tg-01-house-of-blades
}}

''See also: [[Naraka Timeline]]''

== Overview ==
{{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|Naraka, also known as the Cavern of Flame, is a [[Territory]] characterized by extreme heat, darkness, and an acrid, smoky atmosphere. It serves as a realm of law and order where travelers are driven to uphold high moral principles and seek justice. The environment is physically punishing, containing massive caverns and intense heat that can be lethal without specific protections. To prepare for this environment, initiates consume fruit from the obsidian tree to gain heat resistance, with the level of protection serving as a measure of their potential power. [[Travelers]] can access this realm through specific gates, though these portals cannot be opened at just any location without risking death. [[The Territory]] is home to various inhabitants, including the [[Gar]]’rosh lizard-men, and features creatures such as carnivorous lizard-beasts. Naraka is also associated with the [[Naraka Incarnation]], a being composed of volcanic stone and ash with hair made of flame. Becoming an [[Incarnation]] is a difficult process requiring one to simultaneously desire and flee from justice.}}

== House of Blades ==
{{spoiler|book=tg-01-house-of-blades|Naraka is a territory belonging to [[Grandmaster Naraka]] that serves as a source for ferocious creatures and contains various black pits. This realm can be traveled through via specific waypoints used for fast travel, and it is associated with a stripe of [[Travelers]] who wear burnt red robes. The region contains red stones characterized by a gritty, sandy outer coating similar to fine ash. Furthermore, power can be pulled from Naraka, and the group itself is known to send reinforcements.}}

{{spoiler|book=tg-01-house-of-blades|[[Naraka]] is a place from which ferocious creatures originate.<ref name="tg-01-house-of-blades-c2">''House of Blades'', chapter 2</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-01-house-of-blades|[[Naraka]] is a place described as having black pits.<ref name="tg-01-house-of-blades-c2" />}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-01-house-of-blades|[[Naraka]] has waypoints used for fast travel.<ref name="tg-01-house-of-blades-c4">''House of Blades'', chapter 4</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-01-house-of-blades|[[Naraka]] is the name of [[Grandmaster Naraka]]'s [[Territory]].<ref name="tg-01-house-of-blades-c7">''House of Blades'', chapter 7</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-01-house-of-blades|[[Naraka]] is a place one can Travel through.<ref name="tg-01-house-of-blades-c16">''House of Blades'', chapter 16</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-01-house-of-blades|The red stone of [[Naraka]] has a gritty, sandy outer coating like fine ash.<ref name="tg-01-house-of-blades-c16" />}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-01-house-of-blades|[[Naraka]] is a stripe of [[Traveler]] associated with burnt red robes.<ref name="tg-01-house-of-blades-c18">''House of Blades'', chapter 18</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-01-house-of-blades|[[Naraka]] is a place from which power can be pulled.<ref name="tg-01-house-of-blades-c20">''House of Blades'', chapter 20</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-01-house-of-blades|[[Naraka]] is a group that sends reinforcements.<ref name="tg-01-house-of-blades-c21">''House of Blades'', chapter 21</ref>}}

== The Crimson Vault ==
{{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|Naraka is a [[Damascan Traveler]] and a blond woman who possesses the ability to summon fire from the [[Furnace of Judgment]]. Known as the Cavern of Flame, she represents justice, law, and order while wearing red-tinted glasses and blood-red robes. As one of the [[Travelers]] who attacked [[Simon]]'s parents, she is associated with a territory that [[Simon]] intends to never visit again. This location serves as a place from which incursions may come and is inhabited by flaming black lizard-things.}}

{{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|[[Naraka]] is a blond woman who can summon fire from the [[Furnace of Judgment]].<ref name="tg-02-crimson-vault-c1">''The Crimson Vault'', chapter 1</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|[[Naraka]] is a type of [[Traveler]].<ref name="tg-02-crimson-vault-c3">''The Crimson Vault'', chapter 3</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|[[Naraka]] was one of the [[Travelers]] who attacked [[Simon]]'s parents.<ref name="tg-02-crimson-vault-c6">''The Crimson Vault'', chapter 6</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|[[Naraka]] is known as the Cavern of Flame.<ref name="tg-02-crimson-vault-c6" />}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|[[Naraka]] represents law and order.<ref name="tg-02-crimson-vault-c6" />}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|[[Naraka]] wears red-tinted glasses.<ref name="tg-02-crimson-vault-c7">''The Crimson Vault'', chapter 7</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|[[Naraka]] is a place from which incursions may come.<ref name="tg-02-crimson-vault-c10">''The Crimson Vault'', chapter 10</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|[[Naraka]] is a location.<ref name="tg-02-crimson-vault-c10" />}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|[[Naraka]] [[Travelers]] wear blood-red robes.<ref name="tg-02-crimson-vault-c12">''The Crimson Vault'', chapter 12</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|[[Naraka]] is a [[Territory]] that [[Simon]] intends to never visit again.<ref name="tg-02-crimson-vault-c17">''The Crimson Vault'', chapter 17</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|[[Naraka]] is a [[Damascan Traveler]].<ref name="tg-02-crimson-vault-c19">''The Crimson Vault'', chapter 19</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|[[Naraka]] is a place containing flaming black lizard-things.<ref name="tg-02-crimson-vault-c20">''The Crimson Vault'', chapter 20</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|[[Naraka]] represents justice.<ref name="tg-02-crimson-vault-c24">''The Crimson Vault'', chapter 24</ref>}}

== City of Light ==
{{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|Naraka is one of the nine [[Territories]] and a location that the group has previously traveled through. This territory can be entered via a red Gate, and it is home to a pack of dogs. Within this realm, an old woman and several guards have remained, and [[Indirial]] was once ordered to enter the area. The name also refers to a Grandmaster who wears traditional dark red robes and possesses knowledge of what patrols the streets between sundown and sunup. This figure is a deity or force that can be invoked to judge and punish, though [[Alin]] had not seen them all day.}}

{{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|[[Naraka]] will judge and punish.<ref name="tg-03-city-of-light-c2">''City of Light'', chapter 2</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|[[Naraka]] is someone [[Alin]] has not seen all day.<ref name="tg-03-city-of-light-c2" />}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|[[Naraka]] is a deity or force that can be invoked.<ref name="tg-03-city-of-light-c4">''City of Light'', chapter 4</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|[[Grandmaster Naraka]] knows what patrols the streets from sundown to sunup.<ref name="tg-03-city-of-light-c7">''City of Light'', chapter 7</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|[[Grandmaster Naraka]] wears traditional dark red robes.<ref name="tg-03-city-of-light-c9">''City of Light'', chapter 9</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|[[Naraka]] is a Grandmaster.<ref name="tg-03-city-of-light-c9" />}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|[[Indirial]] ordered to go into [[Naraka]].<ref name="tg-03-city-of-light-c10">''City of Light'', chapter 10</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|The old woman and the guards remained in [[Naraka]].<ref name="tg-03-city-of-light-c11">''City of Light'', chapter 11</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|[[Naraka]] is one of the nine [[Territories]].<ref name="tg-03-city-of-light-c19">''City of Light'', chapter 19</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|A red Gate could lead into [[Naraka]].<ref name="tg-03-city-of-light-c21">''City of Light'', chapter 21</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|[[Naraka]] is a [[Territory]] that the group has traveled through before.<ref name="tg-03-city-of-light-c25">''City of Light'', chapter 25</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|[[Naraka]] has a pack of dogs.<ref name="tg-03-city-of-light-c27">''City of Light'', chapter 27</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|[[Rhalia]] kept [[Shai]] in the City to keep her safe from [[Naraka]].<ref name="tg-03-city-of-light-c27" />}}

== Notable quotes ==
* {{spoiler|book=tg-01-house-of-blades|“Then we must act now. If we Travel through Naraka, surely we can make it.”<ref name="tg-01-house-of-blades-c16" />}}
* {{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|“No, just regular Travelers. One of them was Naraka, I think, and the other one summoned this glowing mist.”<ref name="tg-02-crimson-vault-c6" />}}
* {{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|“Into Naraka,” Indirial ordered, not turning from the door.<ref name="tg-03-city-of-light-c10" />}}
* {{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|“Do we have to go through Naraka again?” Simon asked.<ref name="tg-03-city-of-light-c25" />}}

== Notes ==
<references />

{{Traveler's Gate nav}}

[[Category:Locations]]
[[Category:House of Blades]]
[[Category:The Crimson Vault]]
[[Category:City of Light]]
[[Category:Traveler's Gate]]
[[Category:Traveler's Gate locations]]
[[Category:Nations and empires]]
